verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 (HSP60) is canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperone, but it has well-documented non-baseline surface display: (a) tumor cells present HSP60 on the outer plasma membrane leaflet (cancer ecto-chaperone), recognized by antibodies and exploited in preclinical immunotherapy; (b) stressed and apoptotic/pre-apoptotic cells externalize HSP60 to the cell surface as a danger signal engaging innate immune receptors; (c) infected cells also show surface HSP60. This is the classic cell-state-induced ecto-chaperone pattern (parallel to HSP70/GRP78/calreticulin). Not lysosomal exocytosis, not a stable TM-partner tether, not germline-restricted, not baseline dual localization — fits cell_state_induced (stress/cancer/cell-death).
predicted_key_uncertainty: Robustness and tumor-selectivity of the ecto-HSP60 pool vs. mitochondrial dominance; whether surface display is sufficient for binder access in vivo.
